# `dec` sequences

<!-- Generated by `qdb generate reference` from `db/dec.toml`. Do not edit by hand. -->

## DEC Save Cursor (`dec.cursor.save`)

DECSC preserves cursor and DEC presentation state on compatible terminals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC 7`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/cursor_save.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/cursor_save.seq)

## DEC Restore Cursor (`dec.cursor.restore`)

DECRC restores state saved by DECSC.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC 8`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/cursor_restore.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/cursor_restore.seq)

## DECSTBM Set Scroll Region (`dec.screen.scroll_region.set`)

Coordinates are 1-indexed protocol rows.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI top ; bottom r`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/scroll_region_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/scroll_region_set.seq)

## DECSTBM Reset Scroll Region (`dec.screen.scroll_region.reset`)

Resets top/bottom margins to the terminal viewport.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI r`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/scroll_region_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/scroll_region_reset.seq)

## DECSLRM Set Left/Right Margins (`dec.screen.left_right_margin.set`)

Some terminals require DEC private mode 69 before DECSLRM affects margins.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI left ; right s`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/left_right_margin_set.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/left_right_margin_set.seq)

## DECSLRM Reset Left/Right Margins (`dec.screen.left_right_margin.reset`)

This is DEC margin reset syntax, distinct from SCO-style save cursor in some profiles.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI s`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/left_right_margin_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/left_right_margin_reset.seq)

## DEC Index (`dec.screen.index`)

IND moves down one line and scrolls inside the active region when needed.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC D`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/index.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/index.seq)

## DEC Reverse Index (`dec.screen.reverse_index`)

RI moves up one line and reverse-scrolls inside the active region when needed.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC M`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/reverse_index.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/reverse_index.seq)

## DEC Next Line (`dec.screen.next_line`)

NEL moves to the next line using the terminal's newline semantics.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC E`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/next_line.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/next_line.seq)

## DECSTR Soft Reset (`dec.screen.soft_reset`)

Soft reset is terminal-wide state mutation; use deliberately.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `CSI ! p`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/soft_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/soft_reset.seq)

## DECALN Screen Alignment Test (`dec.screen.alignment`)

Diagnostic command that fills the viewport with E characters.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC # 8`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/screen_alignment.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/screen_alignment.seq)

## RIS Full Reset (`dec.screen.full_reset`)

Full reset may clear broad terminal state depending on the emulator profile.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC c`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/full_reset.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/full_reset.seq)

## Designate G0 US ASCII (`dec.charset.g0_us_ascii`)

SCS designates US ASCII into G0.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC ( B`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/charset_g0_us_ascii.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/charset_g0_us_ascii.seq)

## Designate G1 DEC Special Graphics (`dec.charset.g1_special_graphics`)

DEC special graphics is the classic line-drawing set.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC ) 0`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/charset_g1_special_graphics.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/charset_g1_special_graphics.seq)

## Designate G3 DEC Supplemental (`dec.charset.g3_supplemental`)

DEC Supplemental uses the percent intermediate and final byte 5 in VT manuals.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC + % 5`
- References: [VT320 Programmer Reference Manual, Appendix E: Control Functions](https://vt100.net/docs/vt320-uu/appendixe.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/charset_g3_supplemental.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/charset_g3_supplemental.seq)

## Locking Shift G0 (`dec.charset.ls0`)

Maps G0 into GL.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `SI`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/locking_shift_g0.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/locking_shift_g0.seq)

## Locking Shift G1 (`dec.charset.ls1`)

Maps G1 into GL.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `SO`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/locking_shift_g1.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/locking_shift_g1.seq)

## Single Shift G2 (`dec.charset.ss2`)

Uses G2 for the next printable character only.

- Direction: host-to-terminal
- Syntax: `ESC N`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/single_shift_g2.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/single_shift_g2.seq)

## Single Shift G3 (`dec.charset.ss3`)

Uses G3 for the next printable character only; fixture includes the affected byte because ESC O also prefixes SS3 key reports.

- Direction: bidirectional
- Syntax: `ESC O C`
- References: [XFree86 Control Sequences](https://www.xfree86.org/current/ctlseqs.html) (retrieved 2026-07-06)
- Fixtures: [`fixtures/dec/single_shift_g3.seq`](../../../fixtures/dec/single_shift_g3.seq)
